content.problemStatement: Write a C program that takes three integers as input and determines the largest among them. Print the largest number.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space-separated integers denoting the three numbers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a line in the format "Largest: X" where X is the greatest of the three given integers.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The three numbers are 1, 2 and 3; the greatest is 3.
Among 10, 5 and 8 the maximum value is 10.
All three numbers are equal, so the largest is 7.

solution.approach: For each test case we simply compare the three numbers. Initialise the maximum with the first number and update it if the second or third number is larger. This runs in O(1) per test case and uses O(1) extra space. The algorithm is straightforward and works for all allowed values because the numbers fit in a 32-bit signed integer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case
